summary: Marked src/melee/gm/gmopening.c as matching and fixed the full-link small-data layout issue that appeared when linking it. The PR body attributes the mismatch to an external SDA reference to gm_804D67EC: the symbol name was correct, but after gmopening was linked its final .sbss offset was wrong. The source fix reordered the gmopening .sbss declarations around gm_804D67EC, gm_804D67F0, and gm_804D67F4 and made gm_804D67F4 non-static so linker layout matched the target. The symbols map was regenerated/adjusted for related local/global data naming and labels.
what_changed: In configure.py, changed Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gmopening.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/gm/gmopening.c"), linking gmopening.c into the matching build.
In gmopening.c, reordered declarations for gm_804D67EC, gm_804D67F0, and gm_804D67F4 to force the expected .sbss placement.
Changed gm_804D67F4 from static HSD_GObj* to non-static HSD_GObj*, which the PR body says was required for compiler/linker small-data layout.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt for several symbol-scope/name corrections, including gmopening-related .data labels such as unused_floats, @191, @192, @201, and a ...data.0 label.
Added a local ...bss.0 label at .bss:0x80480B38 in symbols.txt.
Converted several previously global-looking autogenerated address names in item/fighter/game-mode data sections to local @NNN symbols where the map now treats them as local constants.
smart_moves: Diagnosed the failure as a full-link SDA layout problem rather than a function codegen problem: fn_801A1498 referenced gm_804D67EC by name, but the final small-data offset was wrong after gmopening became matching.
Fixed the owning translation unit’s .sbss layout instead of patching the external reference, preserving correct ownership for gm_804D67EC.
Used declaration order and linkage/staticness as matching levers for small-data layout, specifically making gm_804D67F4 non-static to align the linker result.
Kept the configure.py matching flip in the same PR as the layout/symbol-map adjustments, so the file could be linked without introducing a full-link mismatch.
decomp_lessons: When marking a file Matching, verify not just per-object matching but also final linked SDA/.sbss offsets, especially for symbols referenced by other translation units.
Small-data symbols can have the right symbol name but the wrong final address if static/global ordering changes after a file is linked.
For .sbss layout, C declaration order alone may not be enough; linkage class can affect how the compiler/linker emits or orders objects.
A non-static declaration may be required for an address-owned object if an external reference or linker layout depends on global visibility.
Regenerate or audit symbols.txt after linking a new object, because local constants may need @NNN names and explicit ...data/...bss labels to reflect actual compiler output.

Evidence for the PR body's claim about adding gm_803DBFCC[] = "MvOpen.mth" is weak in the provided diff excerpt: symbols.txt instead shows gm_803DBFCC at 0x803DBFCC changed to local @201, and gmopening.c only shows the .sbss declaration reorder.
naming_conventions: Use gm_804Dxxxx-style names for game-mode global/small-data symbols only when they are actually global or externally meaningful.

Use compiler-style @NNN names for local string/float/double constants in symbols.txt when the symbol is local, as seen with @191, @192, @201, @355, @356, @174, @175, @179, @180, @182, @184, @267, and @268.
Use ...data.0 and ...bss.0 labels to mark local section labels at data/bss boundaries when needed for map fidelity.
Use descriptive local names where known, such as unused_floats for the local float block at .data:0x803DBF78, instead of an address-only gm_ name.
Do not keep address-prefixed it_ or ftKb_Init_ names as globals if the symbol map evidence says they are local constants; convert them to @NNN local names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Treat full-link mismatch reports involving SDA as layout bugs in the owning source file before changing callsites or references.
Align .sbss by adjusting declaration order around small-data globals.
Adjust static versus non-static storage duration/linkage to influence small-data layout and external visibility.
Audit final offsets for gm_804D67EC, gm_804D67F0, and gm_804D67F4 after the Matching flip.
Use symbols.txt scope and label updates to preserve exact data sizes, alignment, and padding for linked data sections.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 linked code increased to 46.77% (+0.06%, +2164 bytes) and linked data increased to 35.33% (+0.06%, +744 bytes), with 'No changes' otherwise.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs where marking a file Matching caused an SDA or .sbss final-link mismatch.
Search for gm_804D67EC references, especially fn_801A1498, to understand cross-translation-unit ownership.
Look for other gmopening.c symbol-map changes involving gm_803DBFCC or "MvOpen.mth" to verify the PR body's named-data-symbol claim.
Search for uses of making a variable non-static to fix .sbss or small-data ordering in other melee decomp PRs.
